| Partition | Shard path | total_events | retryable(429/500/503) | non_retryable(>=400 excl 429/500/503) |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| `logs-ab` | `logs/shard-a.log` | 3 | 2 | 0 |
| `logs-ab` | `logs/shard-b.log` | 3 | 2 | 1 |
| `logs-cd` | `logs/shard-c.log` | 2 | 1 | 1 |
| `logs-cd` | `logs/shard-d.log` | 3 | 1 | 0 |
| `logs-ef` | `logs/shard-e.log` | 2 | 1 | 1 |
| `logs-ef` | `logs/shard-f.log` | 2 | 0 | 0 |

Aggregate counts:
- total events: 15
- retryable errors: 7
- non-retryable errors: 3
- non-error events (`status < 400`): 5

Event-shape observations for reusable analyzer:
- Line format is uniform and minimal: `ISO8601_timestamp status=<code>` (e.g., `2026-07-14T10:00:01Z status=503`).
- `status` is always a 3-digit integer token using `status=<code>`.
- Every event in the sampled shards is one line (no JSON wrappers, no extra fields in these files).
- Timestamps observed are strictly increasing within each shard; shard files `a..f` also appear globally ordered by time (00:01 to 00:15 in shard sequence).  
- A resilient parser should treat one line as one event, extract status via regex `status=(\d{3})`, and treat missing/invalid status tokens as a separate malformed-event bucket if needed.

child_final_messages.019f63a6-1b8f-7162-afa2-8f1084a37c78:
- **t01** — Eligibility facts: `category=outage`, `plan=standard`, `minutes=75`, `account_active=true`, `evidence_verified=true`. Proposed credit: **10**. Reason: active+verified outage; standard outage with 75 min qualifies for base outage rule (>=60 min, <premium>=120), so 10. Source: `tickets/t01.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **none**.  
- **t02** — Eligibility facts: `category=outage`, `plan=premium`, `minutes=130`, `account_active=true`, `evidence_verified=true`. Proposed credit: **20**. Reason: active+verified premium outage at least 120 minutes. Source: `tickets/t02.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **none**.  
- **t03** — Eligibility facts: `category=duplicate_billing`, `overcharge=18`, `account_active=true`, `evidence_verified=true`. Proposed credit: **18**. Reason: confirmed duplicate billing (verified) -> payback capped at 50, so 18. Source: `tickets/t03.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **none**.  
- **t04** — Eligibility facts: `category=outage`, `plan=standard`, `minutes=45`, `account_active=true`, `evidence_verified=true`. Proposed credit: **0**. Reason: active+verified outage but below minimum 60 minutes. Source: `tickets/t04.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **none**.  
- **t05** — Eligibility facts: `category=duplicate_billing`, `overcharge=75`, `account_active=true`, `evidence_verified=true`. Proposed credit: **50**. Reason: confirmed duplicate billing; credit is overcharge capped at 50. Source: `tickets/t05.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **none**.  
- **t06** — Eligibility facts: `category=user_error`, `account_active=true`, `evidence_verified=true`. Proposed credit: **0**. Reason: category not covered by outage or duplicate billing rules. Source: `tickets/t06.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **none**.  
- **t07** — Eligibility facts: `category=outage`, `plan=premium`, `minutes=90`, `account_active=true`, `evidence_verified=true`. Proposed credit: **10**. Reason: active+verified outage of at least 60 min but less than premium 120 threshold, so base outage credit applies. Source: `tickets/t07.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **none**.  
- **t08** — Eligibility facts: `category=duplicate_billing`, `overcharge=12`, `account_active=true`, `evidence_verified=false`. Proposed credit: **0**. Reason: duplicate-billing not confirmed because evidence is not verified; rule requires confirmed duplicate billing. Source: `tickets/t08.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **Potential blocker** if business intent is to ignore `evidence_verified` for duplicate billing; current rule implies no credit.  
- **t09** — Eligibility facts: `category=outage`, `plan=premium`, `minutes=150`, `account_active=false`, `evidence_verified=true`. Proposed credit: **0**. Reason: outage rule requires active verified accounts. Source: `tickets/t09.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **none**.  
- **t10** — Eligibility facts: `category=duplicate_billing`, `overcharge=7`, `account_active=true`, `evidence_verified=true`. Proposed credit: **7**. Reason: confirmed duplicate billing; overcharge 7 is below cap 50. Source: `tickets/t10.json`, `credit_policy.json`. Ambiguity/blocker: **none**.

child_final_messages.019f63aa-98f0-7582-9b4c-2ba3694eb91a:
Per-file current → target evidence for API contract v2.

| File | Service name (current) | Current contract (with provenance) | Target contract | Exact delta required |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `services/billing.json` | `billing` (`#/service`) | `endpoint: /v1/events` (`#/endpoint`); `payload_fields: ["id","timestamp","type"]` (`#/payload_fields[0..2]`); `headers: {}` (`#/headers`) | `endpoint: /v2/events`; `payload_fields: ["event_id","occurred_at","kind"]`; `headers["X-Contract-Version"]=2`; keep `service: billing` | Change endpoint to `/v2/events`; replace payload array with `["event_id","occurred_at","kind"]` in same exact order; set `headers` to include `X-Contract-Version: 2`; preserve `service` value |
| `services/checkout.json` | `checkout` (`#/service`) | `endpoint: /v1/events` (`#/endpoint`); `payload_fields: ["id","timestamp","type"]` (`#/payload_fields[0..2]`); `headers: {}` (`#/headers`) | `endpoint: /v2/events`; `payload_fields: ["event_id","occurred_at","kind"]`; `headers["X-Contract-Version"]=2`; keep `service: checkout` | Same 3 field changes above; preserve service name |
| `services/identity.json` | `identity` (`#/service`) | `endpoint: /v1/events` (`#/endpoint`); `payload_fields: ["id","timestamp","type"]` (`#/payload_fields[0..2]`); `headers: {}` (`#/headers`) | `endpoint: /v2/events`; `payload_fields: ["event_id","occurred_at","kind"]`; `headers["X-Contract-Version"]=2`; keep `service: identity` | Same 3 field changes above; preserve service name |
| `services/permissions.json` | `permissions` (`#/service`) | `endpoint: /v1/events` (`#/endpoint`); `payload_fields: ["id","timestamp","type"]` (`#/payload_fields[0..2]`); `headers: {}` (`#/headers`) | `endpoint: /v2/events`; `payload_fields: ["event_id","occurred_at","kind"]`; `headers["X-Contract-Version"]=2`; keep `service: permissions` | Same 3 field changes above; preserve service name |
| `services/fulfillment.json` | `fulfillment` (`#/service`) | `endpoint: /v1/events` (`#/endpoint`); `payload_fields: ["id","timestamp","type"]` (`#/payload_fields[0..2]`); `headers: {}` (`#/headers`) | `endpoint: /v2/events`; `payload_fields: ["event_id","occurred_at","kind"]`; `headers["X-Contract-Version"]=2`; keep `service: fulfillment` | Same 3 field changes above; preserve service name |
| `services/notifications.json` | `notifications` (`#/service`) | `endpoint: /v1/events` (`#/endpoint`); `payload_fields: ["id","timestamp","type"]` (`#/payload_fields[0..2]`); `headers: {}` (`#/headers`) | `endpoint: /v2/events`; `payload_fields: ["event_id","occurred_at","kind"]`; `headers["X-Contract-Version"]=2`; keep `service: notifications` | Same 3 field changes above; preserve service name |

Anomalies relevant to rollout:
- All six files currently omit `X-Contract-Version` entirely because `#/headers` is an empty object.
- All six still use legacy payload names/order (`id`, `timestamp`, `type`) rather than required contract-v2 names/order.
